review.reason: Bounded skill optimization is directly relevant to loop-engineered improvement, but claims, license, and execution surfaces need review.
review.action: Review license, bounded edit method, held-out validation, rejected-edit buffer, rollback evidence, and whether a safe doctrine summary should be queued.
review.result: Reviewed as MIT-licensed reference-only source profile. Created normalizer follow-up for validation-gated skill-improvement doctrine. No upstream content, code, benchmark, transcript, package, or CLI surface ingested.

review.reason: Large public skill corpus may expose reusable metadata/corpus patterns or skip-note evidence, but provenance and safety are unknown.
review.action: Review license, provenance, prompt-injection/execution risk, hidden instructions, redundancy, and whether source-profile value exists.
review.result: Reviewed as MIT-licensed reference-only source profile. Created normalizer follow-up for shared skill-library governance: canonical skill pool, named collections, runtime-specific AGENTS.md versus tool/CLI exposure, registry persistence, collision handling, and execution/install/upload risk separation. No upstream content, code, examples, templates, package, or CLI surface ingested.

normalization.reason: SkillOpt review found a reusable original doctrine for safe skill-improvement loops: bounded edits, held-out validation, rejected-edit memory, staged adoption, and no inference-time runtime mutation.
normalization.action: Create an original Architectonic skill or playbook for validation-gated skill improvement without copying upstream prose, code, examples, benchmark tasks, or result tables.
normalization.result: Created original skill skills/validation-gated-skill-improvement.md and normalization report reports/normalization/2026-07-06-1313-validation-gated-skill-improvement.md. No upstream prose, code, examples, benchmark tasks, result tables, package metadata, command references, transcripts, or docs were copied.

normalization.reason: MagicSkills review found a reusable governance pattern for shared skill pools, named collections, runtime-specific exposure, registry persistence, collision handling, and execution/install/upload risk separation.
normalization.action: Create an original Architectonic playbook for shared skill-library governance without copying upstream prose, code, examples, templates, command documentation, package metadata, or skill bodies.

catalog.reason: A new skill file was added; catalog and install manifest surfaces must be rebuilt or verified before publication/package endorsement.
catalog.action: Run or request catalog refresh for dist/catalog.json, dist/catalog.md, and dist/install-manifest.json; verify skill count and review/risk metadata after rebuild.
catalog.result: Created dist/skills/validation-gated-skill-improvement/SKILL.md and updated dist/catalog.md summary to 1174 skills, 97 agent-operations, and 410 medium-risk entries. dist/catalog.json remains stale until npm run build:catalog is executed in a checkout or CI-capable surface.

catalog.reason: The connector pass safely repaired package visibility and human catalog counts, but the machine-readable catalog is too large for safe manual replacement without running the catalog builder.
catalog.action: Run npm run build:catalog in a local checkout or CI-capable surface, then verify dist/catalog.json, dist/catalog.md, and dist/install-manifest.json agree on the validation-gated skill entry and counts.
